title: CRDs, Controllers, Operators, Admission Policies, Webhooks, and Extension Trust · Cluster Case: K8s Admission Policy Webhook
summary: A cluster extension stores an underspecified custom resource, mutates workloads through an unavailable webhook, and upgrades without conversion or rollback evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s admission policy webhook increment in trusted extension API and controller contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Choose built-in admission, ValidatingAdmissionPolicy, validating webhook, or mutating webhook from expressiveness, failure policy, ordering, timeout, availability, and audit needs.
Build or evaluate controllers from watches, work queues, level-based reconciliation, ownership, idempotency, finalizers, status, retries, leader election, and rate limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Admission Policy Webhook · Theory K8s Admission Policy Webhook
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory CRD changes schema without conversion, a webhook fails closed during outage, and an operator hot-loops while fighting field ownership. Bound extension API, defaulting, validation, conversion, admission availability, reconciliation, and uninstall.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the K8s Admission Policy Webhook contract for the supplied data.

For Predict K8s Admission Policy Webhook · Theory K8s Admission Policy Webhook, keep K8s Admission Policy Webhook connected to mechanically reviewable Kubernetes object, status, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example CRD changes schema without conversion, a webhook fails closed during outage, and an operator hot-loops while fighting field ownership. Bound extension API, defaulting, validation, conversion, admission availability, reconciliation, and uninstall. Competency probe: Choose built-in admission, ValidatingAdmissionPolicy, validating webhook, or mutating webhook from expressiveness, failure policy, ordering, timeout, availability, and audit needs. A defensible solution applies K8s Admission Policy Webhook, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to trusted extension API and controller contract.

content.code:
# Worked focus: k8s-admission-policy-webhook — Choose built-in admission, ValidatingAdmissionPolicy, validating webhook, or mutating webhook from expressiveness, failure policy, ordering, timeout, availability, and audit needs.
  # Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-extensions-admission-operators-5
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-extensions-admission-operators-5
    artifact: extension-contract.yaml
    decision: worked k8s extensions admission operators 5
    environment: managed canary cluster with provider-specific load balancing
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      crd: structural-schema-with-status
      storage-version: v1
      conversion: tested
      controller: idempotent-rate-limited-reconcile
      finalizer: external-state-owned
      admission: validating-policy-first
      webhook: bounded-fail-policy
      uninstall: dependency-ordered
    verify: kubectl get crd,validatingadmissionpolicy,validatingwebhookconfiguration -o json
    failure: Reject schema-free CRDs, event-script controllers, unbounded webhooks, fail-open-by-default, or operator deletion as external-state cleanup.
    repair: Version and validate the API, test conversion, bound reconciliation and admission, preserve status, contain failure, and rehearse upgrade and uninstall.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Fail-open admission is always safer because it preserves cluster availability. This fails the K8s Admission Policy Webhook contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
